scenario: Apex Insurance deploys 7 BPMN files and 3 DMN files at application start for 5000 events per day in Camunda 8.8. The team uses the Spring SDK and wants the framework to auto-deploy classpath resources from the resources/bpmn directory on each application start without writing deploy code.
question: Which Camunda 8 Spring SDK annotation triggers classpath resource auto-deployment at startup?

options.text: Annotate a configuration class with @Deployment(resources = "classpath*:bpmn/*.bpmn"); the Spring SDK auto-deploys matched resources at start
Annotate the main class with @EnableZeebeAutoDeploy; the Spring SDK scans the entire classpath for .bpmn files and deploys every match at start
Annotate the main class with @ZeebeDeployment; the Spring SDK scans the default resources/bpmn directory and deploys matched .bpmn files on every restart
Annotate every BPMN file with a Camunda extension element named deploy=auto; the Spring SDK reads the extension and auto-deploys the file at start

a.text: Correct. The Camunda 8 Spring SDK exposes @Deployment(resources = ...) on a configuration class to declare classpath resources for auto-deployment at start.

b.text: Incorrect. There is no @EnableZeebeAutoDeploy annotation in the Camunda 8 Spring SDK.

c.text: Incorrect. The annotation is @Deployment with an explicit resources attribute; @ZeebeDeployment is the legacy name.

d.text: Incorrect. There is no deploy=auto BPMN extension element; deployment is configured on the Spring side.

explanation: The Camunda 8 Spring SDK uses @Deployment(resources = "classpath*:bpmn/*.bpmn") on a configuration bean to auto-deploy at start.
